stem: A proposal enters the grant application pipeline. The probability of passing departmental review is 82%. If the previous stage is passed, the probability of passing external peer review is between 72% and 81% (uncertain due to evaluator variability). If the previous stage is passed, the probability of passing panel discussion is 77%. What is the overall probability that the proposal is funded?

ground_truth.answer: P(funded) ranges from 0.455 to 0.511 as the external peer review pass rate varies from 72% to 81%. Point estimate: 0.486.

solution_steps: The proposal must pass all 3 stages sequentially: P(success) = P(stage 1)×P(stage 2)×P(stage 3).
The uncertainty in the external peer review stage creates a 5.6 percentage point range in the overall success probability.
common_failure_modes: Adding probabilities instead of multiplying for sequential stages
Using a single value for the external peer review rate without acknowledging uncertainty
Not recognizing that uncertainty at one stage propagates through all subsequent stages
